relations.note: Required-bold targets are the mandated members within the overall bolding budget.
Both govern how emphasis (including bold) is applied.

quotes: Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
more than ~15% of running body text in any section
Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**.
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Keep bolding meaningful by reserving it for hierarchy and key takeaways.
rules.rule_text: Bold conveys hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, or the generic name.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).

rules.intent: Guarantee emphasis on the elements that must always stand out.
rules.rule_text: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).

effect.reserved_for: chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup
effect.subject: bold+ALL_CAPS+color_emphasis_combined

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup.
Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Prevent over-emphasized, cluttered strings by capping stacked emphasis devices.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

effect.reserved_for: real hyperlinks (underline); study names, notes, and citations (italic)
effect.subject: underline_and_italic

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Preserve underline and italic as meaningful signals rather than emphasis decoration.
rules.rule_text: Use underlining only for real hyperlinks. Do not use italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
